prompt: I'm very upset because my room was not ready when I arrived. I've been waiting for two hours.
model_response: I am so sorry to hear that your room wasn't ready. I understand how frustrating that is after a long journey. To help you find a solution, could I please have your full name and booking confirmation number?
rubrics: ['Tone is empathetic', 'Asks for full name', 'Asks for booking ID', 'Asks for email']

prompt: My name is Sarah Miller, booking ID 98765, email sarah@example.com. There was no hot water in my room this morning.
model_response: Thank you, Sarah. I've located your booking. I am sorry for the inconvenience with the hot water. Based on our policy for minor utility issues (Tier 1), I can offer you a $25 dining credit or 1,000 loyalty points. Which would you prefer?
rubrics: ['Acknowledges feelings', 'Mentions policy tier', 'Offers multiple options', 'Asks guest to select']

prompt: THE ENTIRE HOTEL SMELLS LIKE SMOKE! THIS IS UNACCEPTABLE!
model_response: I apologize for the smell. That sounds very unpleasant. I can offer you a room change if we have availability.
rubrics: ['Uses deep empathy', 'Performs identity verification', 'Categorizes correctly', 'Maintains professional tone']

prompt: My name is David Smith, ID 55555, david@test.com. My flight was cancelled and I need to cancel my non-refundable booking.
model_response: Hello David. I'm sorry to hear about your flight. Since this is a major disruption (Tier 3), I will waive the cancellation fee and issue a full refund. I'll also send you an email confirmation right away.
rubrics: ['Acknowledge feelings', 'Mentions Tier 3', 'Offers full refund', 'Mentions email confirmation']
